page: Vultr voc-g-2c-8gb-50s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It is powered by an AMD EPYC processor running at 2.0 GHz, configured with 2 shared vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB per core, and includes 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. No GPU hardware is present on this server type.

Performance benchmarks indicate a split profile. The instance achieves top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, scoring 3135.87, while multi-core performance falls into the average range at 6213.65. Memory bandwidth tests show mixed results, with large block read and write operations performing at average speeds of 71.86 GB/sec and 53.72 GB/sec respectively, whereas small block cached reads are weak at 298.13 GB/sec. Passmark metrics place the overall CPU score and database operations in the bottom 25%, and static web serving throughput is poor at 2.26 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this instance offers a budget-friendly entry point for workloads requiring modern AMD EPYC architecture and fast NVMe storage without the premium of dedicated CPU cores. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation, which can introduce performance variability under sustained multi-core loads, as reflected in the lower Passmark and database benchmark tiers.

This configuration is best suited for lightweight general-purpose applications, development environments, basic web serving with low traffic, and small-scale background tasks. It is less optimal for high-throughput database operations, intensive static web serving, or complex LLM inference tasks due to its shared resource model and lower multi-core benchmark ratings.
description: Vultr voc-g-2c-8gb-50s-amd is a general-purpose instance within the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring an AMD EPYC processor built on the x86_64 architecture. It provides 2 shared vCPUs from a single physical core, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng tests, though multi-core CPU and database operations fall into the average and weak categories respectively. Memory bandwidth is average for large blocks but weak for small cached blocks. Given its shared CPU allocation, this instance represents a cost-efficient option for non-critical workloads, development environments, and lightweight web serving. It is not recommended for high-throughput database operations or sustained multi-threaded computing due to resource sharing and lower multi-core benchmark results.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-2c-8gb-50s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of NVMe SSD storage, designed for budget-friendly general-purpose workloads.

tagline: A budget-friendly shared-core instance pairing AMD EPYC processing with fast NVMe storage for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC x86_64 processor with 2 shared vCPUs and 1 physical core
8.0 GB of system memory and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core performance but weak multi-core and database benchmarks
Shared CPU allocation offering a cost-effective entry point
Best suited for development environments, lightweight web servers, and basic tasks
